title: Scroll Area
description: Foldkit-native Base UI Scroll Area primitive preserving root, viewport, content, scrollbar, thumb, corner, orientation, overflow, scrolling, hover, and data-attribute contracts through deterministic model-owned state and geometry helpers.
distinguishes: Foldkit-native Base UI Scroll Area primitive preserving root, viewport, content, scrollbar, thumb, corner, orientation, overflow, scrolling, hover, and data-attribute contracts through deterministic model-owned state and geometry helpers.

runtime.reason: Provides Html, Attribute, and html factory APIs for the Foldkit-native render helper.
Provides Effect Schema for serializable Scroll Area options and deterministic geometry inputs.

development.reason: Origin React runtime is evidence only; installable source is Foldkit-native.
Origin Base UI React primitive is replaced by this Foldkit implementation.
React is origin fixture evidence only, not installable Foldkit source.

deviations.summary: Base UI parity uses a static source-derived origin fixture for root, viewport, content, scrollbar, thumb, corner, data attributes, and CSS variable footprints.
deviations.rationale: Origin Scroll Area relies on live layout measurement and ResizeObserver. The static fixture keeps origin evidence independent of the Foldkit implementation while pinning the deterministic source-derived measured states used by parity.

deviations.summary: Overflow, edge, scrolling, hover, thumb, and corner state are supplied or derived from deterministic metric inputs instead of hidden component state.
deviations.rationale: Foldkit render helpers stay pure. Browser measurement belongs behind commands or future shared measurement infrastructure rather than an ad hoc ResizeObserver inside installable source.

deviations.summary: Track press and thumb drag scroll math is exposed as pure helpers that accept measured sizes.
deviations.rationale: The origin mutates scrollTop and scrollLeft from DOM events. The Foldkit implementation exposes equivalent deterministic calculations so consuming update/command code can own effects.
